refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR uses 'support achieving' which can be ambiguous and potentially implies implementation. Replacing it with 'enable achieving' keeps the focus on the system's capability without specifying how. The FR is atomic as it addresses a single obligation: achieving the energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the energy_per_ton metric (energy_used divided by ton_moved) over the agreed evaluation period and verify it meets or is below the customer-defined target E_target.

candidate_dps: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control
Optimize operational parameters to minimize energy per ton moved
Incorporate adaptive load management to balance energy use
Design energy recovery mechanisms to reuse energy within the system
Establish predictive maintenance to ensure energy-efficient operation
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control

potential_sources: Feedback control tightly linked to energy measurement accuracy
Operational policies may influence control parameters
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates energy measurement and control from other system functions, supporting independence. Coupling risk exists if operational policies are not well integrated but can be managed.

assumptions_risks: Energy measurement can be accurately and reliably obtained in real-time
Operational policies and safety plans do not conflict with energy control strategies
Energy efficiency target E_target is achievable given system constraints
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including operational policy integration as a functional requirement to ensure alignment with energy efficiency control.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for energy management and monitoring within the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time data on energy consumption and system status for monitoring and feedback control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Enables communication with other system modules and external services for control commands and data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as energy monitoring and feedback logically belong here; minimal diffusion since control feedback is internal to energy management.
Moderate containment as vehicle hardware is involved, but logic and monitoring are better centralized elsewhere; diffusion higher due to cross-module dependencies.
Low containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control, not energy monitoring; high diffusion if included here.
Safety may use energy data but does not implement monitoring/control; diffusion high if included here.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and cohesive boundary for implementing real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control, minimizing diffusion and coupling with other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle hardware interfaces may cause some coupling.
If energy data is needed by Safety Services or Teleoperation, interfaces must be well defined to avoid diffusion.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision needed; DP aligns well with NGHE Solution module for containment and implementation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control' directly addresses the FR of enabling achievement of the customer-defined energy efficiency target by providing the necessary monitoring and control mechanisms to meet the acceptance criterion of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle hardware interfaces may cause some coupling.
If energy data is needed by Safety Services or Teleoperation, interfaces must be well defined to avoid diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and containment score (8) with minimal diffusion (2), indicating that the DP is well contained within a single IM boundary. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through measurement of energy_per_ton against the target E_target over the evaluation period, as the NGHE Solution module provides real-time monitoring and feedback control data accessible via defined interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and specific, with well-defined interfaces (Telemetry and Network Link) supporting data exchange and control, minimizing ambiguity in ownership and implementation.

recommended_revisions: No revision needed; the DP aligns well with the NGHE Solution module for containment and implementation.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully with clear alignment and containment. The DP directly addresses the FR with a feasible verification method, and the IM module (NGHE Solution) shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion, indicating a well-contained implementation. No recommended revisions are needed, and the safety criticality has been considered in the evaluation. Therefore, further decomposition into sub-FRs is not necessary at this stage.

assumptions: The DP 'Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control' fully satisfies the FR for energy efficiency target achievement.
The NGHE Solution module is capable of providing the necessary interfaces and data for verification and control.
Safety criticality has been adequately addressed in the current design and implementation.
open_risks: Potential dependency on vehicle hardware interfaces may cause some coupling, but this is managed within the current IM boundaries.
Interfaces with Safety Services or Teleoperation must remain well defined to avoid diffusion.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing energy_per_ton ≤ E_target over the evaluation period.
Interface specifications confirming minimal diffusion and clear ownership within the NGHE Solution module.
